title: Refactor LSP-005 (LSP): Subclass raises a custom exception not defined in the base Mapping interface

detected_issue.description: Subclass raises a custom exception not defined in the base Mapping interface
detected_issue.reasoning: RequestsCookieJar implements the MutableMapping interface. The contract for Mapping.__getitem__ specifies that it should return a value or raise a KeyError. RequestsCookieJar introduces CookieConflictError when multiple cookies match a name, which is an unexpected exception type for callers of the standard Mapping interface, violating the principle that subtypes should not raise new exceptions not raised by the parent.

fix_logic.summary: Replace CookieConflictError with KeyError in __getitem__ to adhere to MutableMapping contract
fix_logic.rationale: The Liskov Substitution Principle requires that subtypes do not raise exceptions not expected by the base interface. MutableMapping.__getitem__ is expected to raise KeyError when a key is missing. By catching the custom CookieConflictError and re-raising it as a KeyError, we ensure that callers of the mapping interface receive the expected exception type.

changes.search_text:
def __getitem__(self, name):
        """Dict-like __getitem__() for compatibility with client code. Throws
        exception if there are more than one cookie with name. In that case,
        use the more explicit get() method instead.

        .. warning:: operation is O(n), not O(1).
        """
        return self._find_no_duplicates(name)

diff:
diff --git a/src/requests/cookies.py b/src/requests/cookies.py
index 7b30370..e8b6c65 100644
--- a/src/requests/cookies.py
+++ b/src/requests/cookies.py
@@ -331,7 +331,10 @@ class RequestsCookieJar(cookielib.CookieJar, MutableMapping):
 
         .. warning:: operation is O(n), not O(1).
         """
-        return self._find_no_duplicates(name)
+        try:
+            return self._find_no_duplicates(name)
+        except CookieConflictError as e:
+            raise KeyError(str(e))

         """Dict-like __setitem__ for compatibility with client code. Throws
